Requirement: WHILE VehSpd is within [0 kph, 250 kph], THE SYSTEM SHALL process the VehSpd value within [50 ms].
Rationale: The requirement uses the generic term 'THE SYSTEM' instead of specifying the exact system/component responsible for processing VehSpd. INCOSE guidelines require clear system identification to ensure traceability and avoid ambiguity in responsibility assignment.

Requirement: IF VehSpd > 250 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_HI_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_HI - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) under a single condition, violating EARS syntax rule that requires one system response per requirement. INCOSE guidelines also emphasize atomic requirements for testability and clarity.

Requirement: IF VehSpd < 0 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_LO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_LO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) in a single statement, violating the atomicity principle which mandates one requirement per functional objective.

Requirement: IF VehSpd is not received within [50 ms], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_TO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_TO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) under a single condition. INCOSE guidelines require each requirement to specify a single, unambiguous system response to ensure testability and avoid ambiguity. Additionally, the transition to safe state lacks a specified time constraint, violating testability principles.

State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement uses 'THE SYSTEM' as a generic term without specifying the exact system/component responsible for the response. INCOSE guidelines and EARS syntax require explicit system naming to ensure unambiguous assignment of responsibility.

Requirement: IF VehSpd CRC is invalid, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_CRC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_CRC - transition to safe state.

Requirement: When VehSpd < 10 kph, THE LKA shall transition to active state
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement follows EARS syntax with a clear trigger (VehSpd < 10 kph), system name (LKA), modality (shall), and system response (transition to active state). It complies with INCOSE guidelines for structural clarity and testability, assuming terms like 'active state' are defined elsewhere in the requirements documentation.
